analysis.device: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
analysis.dtype: fp32 (TF32 tensor path partially engaged on 1x1 GEMM kernels; no bf16/fp16 markers present)

bottleneck.description: The pointwise (1x1, stride 1, groups=1) convolutions are lowered to a register/shared-memory-bloated implicit-GEMM kernel ('Kernel2') that achieves only ~8.3% occupancy. With launch__registers_per_thread = 224-228 and launch__shared_mem_per_block_dynamic = 81.92 KB, each SM can resident only a handful of warps (sm__warps_active.avg = 8.3% of peak). The kernel is latency-bound on Blackwell (smsp__issue_active / eligible_cycles_pct = 16-20%, below the 20% threshold), DRAM throughput sits at only ~26-31%, and Tensor Cores idle ~55% of the time (smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active = 39-45%). A 1x1 conv is mathematically a GEMM over (N*H*W) x Cin x Cout = (16*56*56=50176) x Cin x Cout; the conv lowering forces an implicit im2col tile shape that starves occupancy instead of using a balanced cuBLAS GEMM tile.

transformation.description: Rewrite each 1x1 stride-1 padding-0 groups=1 convolution into an explicit matmul. Reshape the NCHW activation [N,Cin,H,W] to [N*H*W, Cin] (after a channels-last view so the contraction dim is contiguous), multiply by the weight reshaped to [Cin, Cout] (the [Cout,Cin,1,1] weight squeezed and transposed), optionally add the (BN-folded) bias via aten.addmm, then reshape back to [N,Cout,H,W]. cuBLAS selects a balanced GEMM tile that raises occupancy well above 8% and drives the Blackwell Tensor Core path to full utilization. K=Cin=32/64/128 and M=50176 are large enough to be GEMM-efficient.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass, Aten IR, all blocks of the depthwise-separable stack
fx_steps: for node in gm.graph.nodes: match node.target is torch.ops.aten.convolution.default
extract args (input, weight, bias, stride, padding, dilation, transposed, output_padding, groups)
guard: weight is a 4D constant with shape [Cout, Cin, 1, 1] AND stride==[1,1] AND padding==[0,0] AND dilation==[1,1] AND transposed is False AND groups==1
with gm.graph.inserting_before(node): permute input NCHW->NHWC via aten.permute(input, [0,2,3,1]); view to [N*H*W, Cin] via aten.reshape
create weight_2d = aten.reshape(aten.squeeze of weight) to [Cout, Cin] then aten.t to [Cin, Cout]
if bias is not None: mm_out = aten.addmm.default(bias, act_2d, weight_2d) else mm_out = aten.mm.default(act_2d, weight_2d)
reshape mm_out [N*H*W, Cout] -> [N,H,W,Cout] then aten.permute back to NCHW [0,3,1,2]
node.replace_all_uses_with(reshaped); gm.graph.erase_node(node)
gm.graph.lint(); gm.recompile()
transformation.code_hint: Register as a named pass appended to torch._inductor.config.post_grad_custom_pre_pass (torch 2.11). Match torch.ops.aten.convolution.default (NOT F.conv2d). Use gm.graph.inserting_before(node) for the reshape/permute/mm chain and node.replace_all_uses_with for the rewrite.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Neutral-to-slightly-higher DRAM (one extra layout pass) but the GEMM itself drops from ~8% to ~50%+ occupancy; expect the six 1x1-conv kernels to fall from ~178us combined to ~65-90us as latency is hidden and Tensor Cores saturate. Reduction estimate is conservative (~40-50% of Kernel2 time).

optimizations.notes: Largest single opportunity: 'Kernel2' (the 1x1-conv GEMM) accounts for ~20.7% of attributed time at a pathological 8.3% occupancy. Depends on OPT-2 having folded BN bias into the conv bias so the matmul can use aten.addmm and absorb the affine. On Blackwell an optional follow-up is to cast the matmul inputs to TF32/BF16 for the Tensor Core path, but that changes numerics and is left out of this pass.

operators: layer::unique::prologue (30 nodes, contains the triton_poi_fused__native_batch_* chain)

bottleneck.description: Each depthwise-separable block runs an inference batchnorm followed by relu6 as separate full-tensor elementwise passes (triton_poi_fused__native_batch_1/2/3/4, grids 1568/3136/12544/[98,128]). At inference these BNs are static affine transforms (scale*x+shift) that read and rewrite the entire activation tensor (16xCx56x56 = up to 12.5M elements) with zero arithmetic intensity. They surface in the unattributed list and inside the prologue aggregate, collectively dozens of microseconds of pure DRAM round-trips. Folding the inference BN affine into the preceding convolution's weight and bias eliminates these kernels entirely and removes one full activation read+write per block.

evidence.kernel: triton_poi_fused__native_batch_* (BN+affine elementwise) and surrounding triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 layout kernels

counters.note: triton_poi_fused_convolution_0: 9.5% SM throughput, 0 bytes written but 6.43 read -> low-utility pack/normalize pass

transformation.description: Conv-BN folding for inference. Find each aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training (or aten.batch_norm in inference mode) whose sole producer is an aten.convolution.default. Fold the BN affine (gamma/sqrt(var+eps)) into the convolution weight along the output-channel axis and fold (beta - gamma*mean/sqrt(var+eps)) into the convolution bias, then delete the BN node and rewire its users to the conv output. With BN removed, the trailing relu6 (aten.hardtanh.default min=0 max=6) becomes a single pointwise op that Inductor fuses into the conv epilogue, so the standalone normalize kernels disappear.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass, Aten IR, before OPT-1 rewrites convolutions to matmuls
fx_steps: for node in gm.graph.nodes: match node.target in {torch.ops.aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default, torch.ops.aten.batch_norm.default} with training arg False
trace node.args[0] to its producer; guard producer.target == torch.ops.aten.convolution.default and len(list(producer.users)) == 1
read constant tensors weight,bias(conv) and weight(gamma),bias(beta),running_mean,running_var,eps from BN
compute scale = gamma / sqrt(running_var + eps); fold conv_weight *= scale.reshape([-1,1,1,1]); fold conv_bias = (conv_bias - running_mean)*scale + beta (allocate conv_bias if None)
write folded constants back as get_attr targets / updated parameters on gm
bn_node.replace_all_uses_with(producer); gm.graph.erase_node(bn_node)
leave the following aten.hardtanh.default (relu6) in place so Inductor epilogue-fuses it into the conv/matmul output
gm.graph.lint(); gm.recompile()
transformation.code_hint: Run this pass FIRST (it mutates conv weight/bias constants). Match torch.ops.aten.convolution.default and torch.ops.aten._native_batch_norm_legit_no_training.default at Aten IR. Because the pass rewrites constant buffers, it must precede any dtype change; here dtype stays fp32 so only ordering vs OPT-1 matters.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Eliminates one full-tensor read+write per BN (up to 12.5M fp32 = ~50MB each) across ~8 BN sites; removes the standalone triton_poi_fused__native_batch_* normalize kernels and collapses relu6 into the producing kernel's epilogue.

optimizations.notes: Standard inference conv-BN folding; very safe numerically (algebraic identity). Must run before OPT-1 so the folded bias is available to aten.addmm in the 1x1->matmul rewrite, and before OPT-3 so layout decisions act on the simplified graph. The 'prologue' aggregate (50.9%) and the per-op cudnn_convolution entries describe the same logical work under two attribution schemes (fused_kernel_double_count flag); impact pct here is taken against attributed total and is conservative.

bottleneck.description: The depthwise 3x3 convolutions execute as conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc (an NHWC-tiled depthwise kernel) but the surrounding graph is NCHW, so Inductor inserts triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 pack/permute kernels (sm__throughput 9.5-9.7%, l1tex 18%, 0% Tensor Core, occupancy ~54%) before each conv to repack NCHW into NHWC. These layout kernels read 6.43 units and write 0 useful output beyond the repacked tensor -- they are pure format-thrash overhead. The depthwise conv itself is genuinely memory-bound (DRAM 58-75%, occupancy 80-87%, 0% Tensor Core, low arithmetic intensity), which is expected for depthwise; the win is removing the repack kernels by making the entire block channels_last so the permutes cancel across the conv->BN->relu6->pointwise chain.

transformation.description: Force channels_last (NHWC) memory format for the convolution subgraph so Inductor stops emitting per-conv NCHW<->NHWC pack kernels. Insert a single aten.clone with memory_format=torch.channels_last at the block input (or tag conv nodes' input/output meta to channels_last) and let the contiguous-format propagation cancel the intermediate permutes; the depthwise kernel already wants NHWC, so the only surviving layout op is one conversion at the network boundary instead of one per conv.
transformation.location: Inductor post_grad_custom_pre_pass, Aten IR, applied to the chain of aten.convolution.default nodes after OPT-2 folding
fx_steps: identify the contiguous span of aten.convolution.default nodes (depthwise groups==Cin and the OPT-1-rewritten pointwise matmuls) forming each block
at the first conv input in the span: with gm.graph.inserting_before(first_conv): insert aten.clone.default(input, memory_format=torch.channels_last)
rewrite first_conv input to the cloned tensor; set node.meta['memory_format'] hints to channels_last on the spanned conv outputs so Inductor's layout pass keeps NHWC
at the span output: rely on downstream contiguous requirement to insert at most one conversion back, instead of one permute per conv
gm.graph.lint(); gm.recompile()
transformation.code_hint: Match torch.ops.aten.convolution.default and torch.ops.aten.clone.default. Channels_last propagation is an Inductor layout heuristic, so verify with the inductor debug dir that the triton_poi_fused_convolution_0 pack kernels actually disappear; if Inductor re-inserts them, fall back to wrapping the model input in .to(memory_format=torch.channels_last) at the eager boundary.

estimated_impact.memory_traffic_reduction: Removes per-conv NCHW<->NHWC repack passes (each a full-tensor read+write at ~9.5% SM throughput), replacing up to ~6 pack kernels with a single boundary conversion.

optimizations.notes: Channels_last is the correct format for depthwise convs and removes the format-thrash kernels, but Inductor's layout optimizer may already partially handle this and could re-insert conversions; hence medium confidence (also capped at medium because web/doc MCP verification was unavailable). Depends on OPT-2 so the simplified (BN-folded) graph is what the layout pass sees. The depthwise conv kernels themselves are memory-bound by nature and are not the target -- only the surrounding repack overhead is.

global_notes: 2 operators below 1% threshold omitted (combined ~1.37% of attributed time): aten::cudnn_convolution op_id=6 (depthwise 3x3 32ch, 5856ns) and op_id=33 (depthwise 3x3 32ch, 5920ns).

ASSUMPTION: knowledge/hardware-limits.md was not accessible in this environment. RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition treated as GB202-class (sm_120, ~188 SMs). warp_cycles_per_instruction is null throughout (removed on Blackwell), so eligible_cycles_pct < 20 was used as the latency-bound indicator -- the 1x1 GEMM kernels (eligible 16-20%) qualify as latency-bound.

ASSUMPTION: MCP tools (context7, exa-search, memory, sequential-thinking) were unavailable; FX API patterns and transform validity drawn from internal knowledge. Per fallback policy, the one heuristic transform (OPT-3 channels_last) is capped at medium confidence.

EDGE CASE fused_kernel_double_count: the 'layer::unique::prologue' aggregate (50.9%, 30 kernels, Triton-compiled first block) and the per-op aten::cudnn_convolution entries (NVTX/correlation attributed) describe the SAME logical convolutions under two attribution schemes. The time-budget pcts are therefore not strictly additive; impact estimates are expressed against the attributed total and kept conservative. The bottleneck MECHANISM (8% occupancy 1x1 GEMM, BN full-tensor passes, NCHW/NHWC repack) is consistent across both views, so optimization ranking is robust to the double count.

EDGE CASE dynamic_shapes: 'Kernel2' durations span 13696-48480ns and 'conv2d_c1_k1_nhwc' 5856-16575ns (CoV > 50%), but the variance is driven by genuinely different per-block problem sizes (channel counts 32/64/128/256), not by recompilation under changing shapes. No guard-recompile churn implied.

EDGE CASE warm_up_inflation: the 'prologue' operator bundles 30 kernels spanning multiple conv blocks under one NVTX range (first-iteration capture), inconsistent with clean per-iteration kernel counts. Absolute durations are additionally 2-5x inflated by ncu counter collection; all durations were used only for relative ranking within this profile.

Dependency DAG: OPT-2 (conv-BN fold) -> OPT-1 (1x1 conv -> addmm, needs folded bias) and OPT-2 -> OPT-3 (layout on simplified graph). OPT-1 and OPT-3 are independent of each other. No cycles. Apply order: OPT-2, then OPT-1, then OPT-3.

Across all operators, smsp__pipe_tensor_cycles_active is 0 on every depthwise/layout kernel and only 39-45% on the 1x1 GEMM kernels, while the device is a Tensor-Core-rich Blackwell part -- the workload is overwhelmingly leaving Tensor Cores idle. OPT-1 is the primary lever to engage them.
